answer:
The inflection point occurs where the second derivative changes sign. For \( f(x) = \frac{10 \ln x}{x^2} \), the second derivative simplifies to \( f''(x) = \frac{10(6\ln x - 5)}{x^4} \). Setting \( 6\ln x - 5 = 0 \) gives \( \ln x = \frac{5}{6} \), so \( x = e^{5/6} \approx 2.301 \).

\boxed{J}
